input.final_summary: CI is currently checks-only in GitHub Actions, while deployment configuration is mostly Cloudflare Workers Builds documentation plus local Wrangler scripts. Web and API both define staging/production Wrangler environments, but no repo automation maps master to staging or production to production yet.

content.text:
## Relevant Files
- `/Users/duhl/git/anedot-one/.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- `/Users/duhl/git/anedot-one/package.json`
- `/Users/duhl/git/anedot-one/apps/web/package.json`
- `/Users/duhl/git/anedot-one/apps/web/wrangler.jsonc`
- `/Users/duhl/git/anedot-one/apps/web/README.md`
- `/Users/duhl/git/anedot-one/apps/api/package.json`
- `/Users/duhl/git/anedot-one/apps/api/wrangler.jsonc`
- `/Users/duhl/git/anedot-one/apps/api/README.md`
- `/Users/duhl/git/anedot-one/turbo.json`
- `/Users/duhl/git/anedot-one/README.md`

## Existing CI/CD Setup
GitHub Actions currently has only one workflow: `CI`. It runs on pushes to `master` and `main`, plus all pull requests. The only job installs dependencies and runs:

```sh
pnpm check
```

There are no GitHub Actions deploy jobs, no Wrangler auth setup, no Cloudflare deploy action, and no branch-specific deployment logic in `.github/workflows`.

Root package scripts include web deploy commands:

```sh
pnpm deploy:web
pnpm deploy:web:preview
```

Those expand to:

```sh
pnpm build:web && pnpm --filter web deploy
pnpm build:web && pnpm --filter web deploy:preview
```

`apps/web/package.json` defines:

```sh
wrangler deploy
wrangler versions upload
```

`apps/api/package.json` defines:

```sh
wrangler deploy --minify
```

There is no root-level `deploy:api` script and no API preview/version upload script.

## Wrangler / Environment Shape
`apps/web/wrangler.jsonc` defines base worker `web` and two Wrangler envs:

```jsonc
"env": {
  "staging": { "name": "web-staging" },
  "production": { "name": "web-production" }
}
```

`apps/api/wrangler.jsonc` defines base worker `api` and two Wrangler envs:

```jsonc
"env": {
  "staging": { "name": "api-staging" },
  "production": { "name": "api-production" }
}
```

Both apps include local Wrangler build commands that run Turbo from the repo root. Docs explicitly note Cloudflare Workers Builds does not run Wrangler `[build]`; the Cloudflare dashboard build command must run Turbo directly.

## Branch / Environment Assumptions
Current repo assumptions appear to be:

- `master` and `main` are CI branches only.
- Cloudflare Workers Builds may be used outside GitHub Actions, based on `README.md`.
- Non-production branches are expected to use `npx wrangler versions upload`.
- Web production local deploy is documented as `pnpm deploy:web`.
- API production deploy is documented only from `apps/api` as `pnpm deploy`.
- Staging/production Wrangler env names exist, but current scripts do not pass `--env staging` or `--env production`.

## Gaps For Desired Deploys
To add staging deploy on `master` and production deploy on `production`, the main gaps are:

- No GitHub Actions deployment workflow exists.
- No workflow currently triggers on `production`.
- Existing deploy scripts deploy the default Wrangler environment unless changed to use `--env staging` / `--env production`.
- API lacks root scripts like `deploy:api`, `deploy:api:staging`, or preview equivalents.
- API deployment needs Cloudflare secrets/vars per environment: `DATABASE_URL`, `LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN`, `CORS_ORIGIN`, `ENVIRONMENT`, `LOG_LEVEL`, and optionally `LOGTAIL_ENDPOINT`.
- Web deployment needs build-time WorkOS vars per environment: `VITE_WORKOS_CLIENT_ID` and `VITE_WORKOS_API_HOSTNAME`.
- Docs are slightly stale: root `README.md` says each app has `wrangler.toml`, but web/api use `wrangler.jsonc`.
- Need to decide whether deployments should live in GitHub Actions or remain in Cloudflare Workers Builds dashboard configuration.
